name: Mashed Potato Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the 1 cup of cooked mashed potatoes in a clean quart container.
2. Add sugar, salt and warm water to the mashed potatoes.
3. Mix well until all ingredients are incorporated.
4. Cover the container with a clean cloth.
5. Place the container in a warm location for 2-3 days or until the mixture ferments, bubbles up, and smells pleasantly sour.
6. Use the starter immediately if it has fermented as desired, or store it in the refrigerator in a clean plastic container.
7. Make sure the container is covered with a small hole punched to allow gases to escape.
8. Sourdough breads made from this starter will not taste any different than other starters and will not have any of the flavor of potatoes.
9. Replenish the starter by adding equal portions of flour and warm water, as per the original recipe.
10. Allow the replenished starter to ferment at room temperature for 8-10 hours before returning it to use.
11. If the starter is not used and replenished once a week, stir down the mixture after 3-4 weeks.
12. Discard half of the starter and replenish with the same recipe as needed.
13. If a clear liquid forms on top of the mixture, simply stir it down and continue to use as needed.
14. A sprinkle of dry yeast may be added if the starter does not appear bubbly or fermented after being in use for some time.
